description_long: This clock tower features a tall, rectangular shaft constructed from a material resembling brick or stone, with metallic or concrete bands reinforcing its structure at intervals. The upper section is capped with a pointed, spire-like roof made of metal, featuring open arches and a small clock face on at least one side. The lower shaft displays some decorative elements, including a spiral motif and what appears to be a relief or mural. The base of the tower is solid and robust, with two symmetrical supports. The overall design is utilitarian with subtle decorative flourishes, suggesting a blend of historical and modern influences. The clock face is small and round, with minimalistic markings.
description: A tall, rectangular brick or stone clock tower with a pointed metal spire roof, decorative bands, and a small round clock face. The structure features subtle decorative elements and a robust base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the clock face, spiral decorative motif, and the most detailed side of the shaft.
description_view_1: Side view with partial visibility of the clock face and spiral motif, showing more of the shaft's texture.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible clock face or decorative motifs, highlighting the reinforcing bands and plain shaft.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to the rear, with reinforcing bands and a plain shaft, no visible clock face.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is small, round, and minimalistic, with no visible numerals or detailed markings. It features two simple hands and a plain border, lacking ornate or decorative elements.
